text:
Once upon a time in a bustling forest lived an old owl named Percival. Percival was unlike any owl you'd ever meet. Firstly, he had an incredible passion for gardening indoors. Perhaps it was thanks to a botanic fascination from observing squirrels outdoors. Every corner of his nest was decorated with geraniums, ivies, and pots of delicate flowers. Secondly, he was remarkably chatty. Perhaps it was just that old wisdom nestled within him, or perhaps Percival had nothing better to do all day. Either way, he filled every hour with stories.

One sunny afternoon, two young birds dared to land on Percival's branch.

"Why do you always talk so late into the evening?" they chirped.

"Just telling tales," Percival replied with a wink. "Like this one time, a fox pretended to drown itself over an ancient, hollow oak tree. I told you how brave it was? Now go swoop onto those worms!"
